methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing phytolith samples from palm species for morphological analysis.

steps.description: Reference material of 24 palm species was collected from the Leiden Herbarium and Hortus Botanicus Amsterdam.
steps.searchable_summary: collect reference material of palm species from specified locations

steps.description: Specimens were sampled for leaves, woody parts, fruits, or seeds, depending on availability.
steps.searchable_summary: sample specimens for various plant parts

steps.description: All samples were processed according to standard laboratory procedures for wet processing.
steps.searchable_summary: process samples using standard wet processing procedures

steps.description: Organic matter and carbonates were removed from the phytolith samples using 33% H2O2, KMnO4, and 10% HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: remove organic matter and carbonates from samples

steps.description: Phytoliths were extracted by heavy liquid flotation using Bromoform at a specific gravity of 2.3.
steps.searchable_summary: extract phytoliths using heavy liquid flotation

steps.description: Phytoliths were mounted on microscope slides using Naphrax and identified using immersion oil at 1000× magnification under a Zeiss Axioscope microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: mount and identify phytoliths on microscope slides

steps.description: The relative abundance of morphotypes for each sample was calculated by counting a total of 300 palm phytoliths per slide.
Phytoliths were counted using morphological descriptions from the literature, using the latest nomenclature.
For each sample, at least 30 phytoliths and for each morphotype at least 3 replicates per species were photographed and morphological characteristics were measured using Image J software.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph and measure morphological characteristics using Image J

methods.description: Measurement of numerical variables for conical and spheroid phytoliths using Image J software.

steps.description: The following numerical variables were measured for conical phytoliths: length (μm), width (μm), number of projections, length of spines (μm), width of spines (μm), distance between spines (μm) and sphericity (length/width).
steps.searchable_summary: measure numerical variables for conical phytoliths

steps.description: For spheroid phytoliths, the numerical variables measured were maximum diameter (μm), number of surficial projections, length of spines (μm), width of spines (μm), distance between spines (μm), sphericity (maximum diameter/perpendicular diameter) and perpendicular diameter (μm).
steps.searchable_summary: measure numerical variables for spheroid phytoliths

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the morphological data collected from phytolith samples.

steps.description: Principal Components Analysis (PCA) was used to simultaneously assess the variability of all morphological measurements across species.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to assess variability of morphological measurements

steps.description: PCA were run separately within general morphotypes (e.g. within conical phytoliths and within spheroid phytoliths).
steps.searchable_summary: run PCA separately for each morphotype

steps.description: Pearson correlation tests were used to see if measured characteristics were correlated within each morphotype separately.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Pearson correlation tests for each morphotype

steps.description: Pairwise comparisons between species were conducted for each variable separately.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pairwise comparisons for each variable

steps.description: Mean differences between the measured morphological characteristics of species were tested using ANOVA and Tukey tests, or Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn tests.
steps.searchable_summary: test mean differences using ANOVA and other tests

steps.description: P-values were adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
The minimum sample size needed to accurately reflect mean values of each morphotype and species was calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate minimum sample size for accurate mean values
